diagnosis: The player spawned on a solid floor with no visible exit path and immediately stalled for ~9 seconds. They then moved right, climbed ladders into a dense vertical shaft filled with repeating 4-tile platform clusters and enemies (types 1, 2, 3, H), but made no forward progress past x≈2700 after 55 seconds. The trace shows repeated up-down oscillation at the same x-positions (e.g., 2188–2290, 2058–2234) indicating the player could not find a consistent route upward. Zero falls and zero deaths confirm the geometry is traversable but the path is opaque — the level reads as a vertical maze with no legible flow, causing the player to quit and rate it 1/5.

lessons.lesson: Vertical shafts built from repeating identical platform clusters without visual or spatial differentiation create navigation ambiguity — players cannot distinguish progress-critical paths from dead ends, leading to oscillation and perceived impossibility.
conditions: platform clusters repeat at uniform spacing
no landmarks, color cues, or structural breaks to mark the critical path
multiple enemy types placed without spatial hierarchy
lessons.outcome: player oscillates vertically at same x-positions for 30+ seconds, fails to locate exit, rates level 1/5
